keyMessages: WCEPS tailors research-based products and services to meet unique education needs.
Pathways programs help educators identify a challenge, choose a pathway, and continue learning through partnership.
WCEPS supports schools and districts with educational resources, assessments, professional learning, program evaluations, and content alignment analyses.
proofPoints: WCEPS is described on the Pathways site as a 501(c)(3) charitable nonprofit organization.
The site says WCEPS extends the impact of University of Wisconsin-Madison educational innovations.
brief.layoutRecommendation: Use a structured one-pager with a summary column, key message cards, evidence-backed proof points, and a bottom CTA band.

headlineOptions: Customized support for schools and districts
subheadOptions: Pathways Powered by WCEPS helps education teams chart a course through research-based resources, professional learning, assessments, program.
WCEPS tailors research-based products and services to meet unique education needs.
WCEPS tailors research-based products and services to meet unique education needs.
copy.body: WCEPS tailors research-based products and services to meet unique education needs. For education leaders, this draft focuses on research-based educational supports and service pathways. WCEPS is described on the Pathways site as a 501(c)(3) charitable nonprofit organization.
bullets: WCEPS tailors research-based products and services to meet unique education needs.
Pathways programs help educators identify a challenge, choose a pathway, and continue learning through partnership.
WCEPS supports schools and districts with educational resources, assessments, professional learning, program evaluations, and content alignment.
WCEPS is described on the Pathways site as a 501(c)(3) charitable nonprofit organization.
The site says WCEPS extends the impact of University of Wisconsin-Madison educational innovations.

fittedCopy.headline: Customized support for schools and districts
fittedCopy.deck: Connect teams with research-based resources, professional learning, assessments, and alignment support.
proofPoints: Tailor research-based products and services to meet unique education needs.
Help educators identify a challenge, choose a pathway, and continue.
Schools and districts with educational resources, assessments, professional learning, program.

compositionTemplate.typographyScale: magazine headline, one deck, short proof rail
compositionTemplate.artPlateTreatment: edge-to-edge editorial art column or band
compositionTemplate.copyPlacement: large type block with compact editorial proof band

designRecipe.hierarchy: magazine one-pager with commanding headline and deck on the left, a compact proof rhythm leading to the CTA, and a premium right-field visual that reinforces customized education support
designRecipe.artDirection: executive education editorial: a warm, credible WCEPS-inspired scene of district leaders and educators collaborating around research, assessment, and implementation pathways, using refined navy, teal, gold, and soft neutral tones with purposeful negative space for app-set copy
designRecipe.placeholderStrategy: shape the right-field imagery and tonal gradients to create a natural left reading zone and lower proof-band cadence; no fake copy, logos, blank cards, boxes, or UI scaffolds
designRecipe.appComposition: app places the official logo top-left, headline and deck in the primary left text zone, proof points in the integrated lower proof band, and the CTA in the proof-band action lockup
promptDirectives: Design a complete premium one-page editorial concept, not a decorative background.
Use the visual crop to guide attention from headline area to proof band and CTA.
Suggest research-based school support through people, pathways, and subtle non-text academic materials.
Do not generate readable text, logos, buttons, contact bars, placeholder boxes, or fake documents.

compositionScore.question: Would I send this to the WCEPS team without apology?
warnings: WCEPS prompt is missing avoid guardrail(s): diploma, fake university logo, mascot.

layoutQa.question: Would I send this layout to the WCEPS team without apology?

renderQa.question: Would I send this rendered artifact to the WCEPS team without apology?

modelQa.question: Would a senior WCEPS reviewer send this artifact without apology?

The art plate is professional and safe, but the educator meeting scene reads as generic institutional collaboration with limited WCEPS/pathways specificity.

imageResults.revisedPrompt:
Create a 1024x1536 portrait full-bleed campaign art plate for a premium institutional education marketing one-pager. No readable text, no logos, no initials, no wordmarks, no marks, no labels, no buttons, no cards, no panels, no charts, no contact bars, no fake UI, no placeholder modules.

Brand mood and palette: warm nonprofit education institution, credible and research-informed, using deep navy #142836, teal #0081A4, aqua #3EB3BD, and grounded green #5EA974 as tonal accents. Editorial, polished, practical, human, finished.

Composition: magazine one-pager art plate with a strong natural type-safe reading zone on the left for app-composed official logo/headline/deck, and a premium visual field on the right. Create subtle tonal gradients and spatial depth so the left third to half is calm, darker/cleaner, and high-contrast for future typography without being an empty panel or blank box. The right field carries the main visual storytelling. Lower portion should have a compact proof-band cadence implied through rhythm, texture, and soft visual anchors only, leaving room for app proof points; do not create boxes or text placeholders.

Subject/scene: authentic editorial photography or refined photorealistic editorial-illustrative atmosphere of district leaders, educators, and education support specialists collaborating around research-informed school support and service pathways in a bright school or district office setting. Diverse adult educators, medium distance, no close-up faces, no detailed hands. They review planning notes, educator resource packets, district strategy materials, and inclusive classroom support materials, but all paper surfaces must be blank or abstract with no legible marks. Suggest research, customized educational support, professional learning, pathways, and inclusive district collaboration through environment, posture, materials, layered surfaces, and visual flow.

Visual hierarchy: guide attention from the upper-left headline area toward the collaborative right-field scene, then down toward the lower proof rhythm. Use layered depth: foreground soft-focus table edge or materials, midground collaborative group, background bright school/office architecture with glass, shelving, pinboard shapes, and soft daylight. Rich visual density on the right, calmer on the left. Avoid generic stock workshop posing and meeting-table cliches; make it feel like an executive education editorial concept from a high-end magazine/ad agency.

Restrictions: absolutely no readable text, letters, numbers, labels, fake paragraphs, book titles, publisher names, email, URLs, citations, logos, seals, badges, arrows, chevrons, mountain-like marks, icons, charts, document layouts, blank modules, CTA-like shapes, social/web/contact card elements. Do not over-describe individual programs or show program-specific branding. Keep important visual energy away from trim edges. Full designed concept, polished color grading, intentional crop, premium atmosphere.

request.topic: research-based educational supports and service pathways
request.notes: Use the parent brand without over-describing individual programs.

sourceEvidence.excerpt: Discover your pathway through WCEPS' research-based educational resources, publications, assessments, professional learning, program evaluations, and content alignment analyses.
sourceEvidence.label: Homepage positioning

sourceEvidence.excerpt: We are a non-profit committed to supporting educators in K-12 and higher education in their mission to support students and create an educational landscape where all students, regardless of their background, can thrive and reach their full potential.
sourceEvidence.label: Nonprofit purpose

sourceEvidence.excerpt: The Wisconsin Center for Education Products and Services is a 501(c)3 charitable non-profit organization with a mission to extend the impact of the University of Wisconsin-Madison's educational innovations and bring research-based, innovative, and customized educational solutions to schools and districts.
sourceEvidence.label: WCEPS mission footer

sourceEvidence.excerpt: As a non-profit organization offering research-based solutions, we strive to listen to what our district colleagues need, understand your challenges, and consider how our programs can help you reach your goals, without pushing things you don't need.
sourceEvidence.label: Contact page tone
